data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T101828-8f353a90/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T101828-8f353a90/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T101828-8f353a90/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T101828-8f353a90/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T101828-8f353a90/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f42dc71a">
    <title>work: auto-commit durable worker audit outputs</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Durable DDx audit outputs still appear as uncommitted local dirt after normal worker attempts. Today the live monitor saw `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` appended and `.ddx/beads.jsonl` outcome rows changed, while the worker continued. The operator-facing behavior must be simple: if DDx writes durable tracked audit state, it commits it; if the state is volatile, it never writes it to tracked files.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:548-571` appends attempt rows to `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` through `attemptmetrics.AppendRow` without staging or committing the metrics file.
- `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:19` defines `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` as the durable per-attempt analytics table, so ignoring it would contradict ADR-026.
- `cli/internal/agent/commit_outcome.go:34-60` handles store mutation errors after outcomes, but successful durable tracker mutations are not wrapped in a transaction that commits the resulting `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`, and related attachment writes.
- Existing bead `ddx-051500ff` covers attachment auto-commit; this bead covers the remaining metrics/outcome tracker writes so the system has one coherent tracked-output policy.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a single DDx-managed tracked-output commit path for worker attempts. After an attempt appends attempt metrics or applies a terminal durable tracker mutation, stage only DDx-managed durable paths that changed (`.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`, and attachment files covered by `ddx-051500ff`) and create one audit commit such as `chore: update tracker (execute-bead <attempt-id>)` or a more specific metrics/outcome message. If the commit fails, stop the worker with operator attention instead of continuing to accumulate dirty tracked state.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not auto-commit arbitrary implementation files.
- Do not change attempt metrics schema.
- Do not duplicate attachment reconciliation work owned by `ddx-051500ff`; compose with it or depend on it.
- Do not hide commit failures behind best-effort warnings.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWorkAttemptMetricsAutoCommitted|TestWorkOutcomeTrackerMutationAutoCommitted|TestWorkStopsWhenDurableAuditCommitFails' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/attemptmetrics ./internal/agent -run 'TestAttemptMetricsAppendLeavesNoDirtyTrackedFile|TestCommitOutcomeDurableMutationUsesAuditCommit' -count=1` passes.
3. A fixture attempt that appends `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` and updates `.ddx/beads.jsonl` exits with `git status --short -- .ddx/beads.jsonl .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl .ddx/attachments` empty after the audit commit.
4. A forced git commit failure leaves the bead unclaimed or parked for operator attention and stops `ddx work --watch`; the worker must not claim another bead while durable audit files are dirty.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:execution, area:metrics, area:bead, kind:bug, operator-ux</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-c191e91a" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="a7cb320de16b1351b0d7c19f22bda0e9a77ccf10" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T101828-8f353a90"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the durable audit auto-commit path for worker attempts. The loop now finalizes metrics plus DDx-managed tracker outputs through one locked commit helper in [durable_audit.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f42dc71a-20260515T101828-8f353a90/cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go), and [execute_bead_loop.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f42dc71a-20260515T101828-8f353a90/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go) stops with operator attention if that commit fails instead of continuing with dirty `.ddx` state. I also wired the shared `work`/`try` entrypoints through the new finalizer in [execute_loop_shared.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f42dc71a-20260515T101828-8f353a90/cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go) and [try.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f42dc71a-20260515T101828-8f353a90/cli/cmd/try.go), and added the regression coverage in the new `cmd`, `internal/agent`, and `internal/attemptmetrics` tests.

Verification passed:
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWorkAttemptMetricsAutoCommitted|TestWorkOutcomeTrackerMutationAutoCommitted|TestWorkStopsWhenDurableAuditCommitFails' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/attemptmetrics ./internal/agent -run 'TestAttemptMetricsAppendLeavesNoDirtyTrackedFile|TestCommitOutcomeDurableMutationUsesAuditCommit' -count=1`
- `cd cli && CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./cmd`
- `lefthook` pre-commit passed on commit

History ended up with two commits because the first pre-commit run exposed an existing `cmd` fixture that needed local git identity once audit commits became real:
- `8bdcdf113` `fix(work): auto-commit durable audit outputs [ddx-f42dc71a]`
- `35c181cb9` `test(cmd): configure work audit fixture git identity [ddx-f42dc71a]`
